history:agent1qgdsv5ft53hjhte3z96ejzhjct:
[{"role": "user", "content": "@agent1qw6pmhqylhn3wrczdmmv9ttul26j4h29zd38ae2xeu90dewnjpzvzpnhqmj Compare Citadel and Jane Street's hiring patterns"}, {"role": "assistant", "content": "Let me try a different approach and pull the data for each firm separately:I see a striking overlap at the very top, with some revealing differences in depth and breadth.

Citadel placed 133 interns in 2024, Jane Street 77. Both firms draw most heavily from the same elite quartet: MIT, Columbia, and Princeton dominate both pipelines. MIT sends 18 to Citadel but 30 to Jane Street, making it nearly 40 percent of Jane Street's entire class. Columbia and Princeton contribute almost identically to both firms, 17 and 15 to 16 interns each.

The divergence appears in the second tier. Citadel casts a wider net: Harvard, Cornell, UPenn, NYU, UIUC, Yale, Duke, Brown, and Georgia Tech all feed meaningful cohorts. Jane Street, by contrast, shows just five universities in the data, adding only Stanford and Caltech beyond the top three. Stanford sends 11 to Jane Street versus 9 to Citadel, and Caltech feeds 4 to each.

Jane Street's pipeline is more concentrated, almost monastic in its focus on a handful of math and CS powerhouses. Citadel recruits with similar prestige but broader geographic and institutional reach, pulling from strong state programs like UIUC and Georgia Tech alongside the Ivies."}, {"role": "user", "content": "@agent1qw6pmhqylhn3wrczdmmv9ttul26j4h29zd38ae2xeu90dewnjpzvzpnhqmj How many UCLA students interned at Jane Street in the same year?"}, {"role": "assistant", "content": "None. Jane Street placed zero UCLA interns in 2024. That's consistent with the hyper-selective, tightly concentrated pipeline I just described: five universities, all with exceptionally strong math and theoretical computer science cultures. UCLA didn't make the cut."}, {"role": "user", "content": "@agent1qw6pmhqylhn3wrczdmmv9ttul26j4h29zd38ae2xeu90dewnjpzvzpnhqmj how many swe interns did UC berkeley place in 2025?"}, {"role": "assistant", "content": "UC Berkeley placed 207 SWE interns in 2025, and Amazon absorbed 73 of them, more than a third of the entire cohort.

The rest spread across big tech and high-growth infrastructure companies: Meta took 27, Google 23, Microsoft 14, Apple 13, and Tesla 11. Nvidia, AWS, Atlassian, Databricks, and a handful of others filled out the tail. Stripe got 4, about what you'd expect given relative headcount.

Berkeley remains a massive SWE factory, and Amazon remains its biggest single employer by far."}, {"role": "user", "content": "@agent1qw6pmhqylhn3wrczdmmv9ttul26j4h29zd38ae2xeu90dewnjpzvzpnhqmj how many swe interns did UC berkeley place in 2024?"}, {"role": "assistant", "content": "UC Berkeley placed 158 SWE interns in 2024, about 75 percent of the 2025 volume.

Amazon still led with 29, but the distribution was more even. Atlassian took 19, AWS 15, Meta 14, Google 13, Microsoft and Apple 12 each. Databricks, Nvidia, and a long tail of big tech and enterprise firms rounded out the rest. Stripe placed 5, matching its 2025 intake.

The jump from 158 to 207 year-over-year suggests either better data coverage in 2025 or a real expansion in Berkeley's SWE intern pipeline, with Amazon capturing most of the growth."}, {"role": "user", "content": "@agent1qw6pmhqylhn3wrczdmmv9ttul26j4h29zd38ae2xeu90dewnjpzvzpnhqmj how many interns did UC berkeley place in 2024?"}, {"role": "assistant", "content": "UC Berkeley placed 392 interns in 2024 across all roles, nearly 2.5 times the SWE-only count.

Amazon remained the top single destination with 67 interns, spanning SWE, business, and other technical roles. But the full picture is much broader: Big Four consulting and accounting firms pulled serious numbers (EY 29, Deloitte 25, PwC 19, McKinsey 12, BCG 6), as did major banks like Wells Fargo with 19.

Tech still dominated: Apple 28, Atlassian 27, Google 26, Meta 25, AWS 20, Databricks 19, Microsoft 15. Nvidia, Tesla, Stripe, Salesforce, LinkedIn, Snowflake, and Palantir all hired cohorts of 5 to 11.

Berkeley feeds every sector: cloud infrastructure, enterprise SaaS, consumer hardware, finance, strategy consulting. It's one of the most versatile pipelines in the country."}, {"role": "user", "content": "@agent1qw6pmhqylhn3wrczdmmv9ttul26j4h29zd38ae2xeu90dewnjpzvzpnhqmj how many interns did UC berkeley place in Amazon in2024?"}, {"role": "assistant", "content": "67 interns from UC Berkeley went to Amazon in 2024. That's consistent with what I told you a moment ago when you asked about Berkeley's overall placement: Amazon was the single largest destination, absorbing about 17 percent of Berkeley's entire intern class that year."}]
